qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Duplicate crack-file chunk uploads bypass server disk quotas
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding covers crack-file quota accounting in server/rpc/rpc-crackstations.go and server/db/helpers.go, while the advisory is a reverse-tunnel close nil dereference in server/handlers/sessions.go:tunnelCloseHandler.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The only real session finding is a crackstation duplicate-chunk quota bypass, not the nil pointer dereference in tunnelCloseHandler when closing reverse tunnels.
evidence: The advisory describes a nil pointer dereference in server/handlers/sessions.go:tunnelCloseHandler where tunnel.SessionID is dereferenced after core.Tunnels.Get(...) returned nil during reverse-tunnel close handling.

The only final qualifying finding targets CrackFileCreate and CrackFileChunkUpload quota accounting in server/rpc/rpc-crackstations.go, server/db/helpers.go, and server/db/models/crackstations.go, with a PoC showing duplicate chunk uploads writing 4096 bytes while reported usage remains 1024.
No final finding report or session artifact ties the reported bug to reverse tunnels, rtunnels, tunnelCloseHandler, or server/handlers/sessions.go.
